golden_categories.name: Basic Email Validation
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating standard email formats and basic rules.

golden_categories.name: Domain-Specific Validation
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses with domain-specific rules.

golden_categories.name: Underscore in Domain
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses with underscores allowed in the domain.

golden_categories.name: UTF8 Local Part Restriction
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses without UTF8 characters in the local part.

golden_categories.name: Display Name Support
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses with optional display names.

golden_categories.name: Required Display Name
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses with mandatory display names.

golden_categories.name: IP Domain Validation
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses with IPs allowed as domains.

golden_categories.name: Blacklisted Characters
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses with restrictions on specific characters in the name.

golden_categories.name: Max Length Restriction
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses with maximum length restrictions.

golden_categories.name: Ignore Max Length
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses with maximum length ignored.

golden_categories.name: Blacklisted Domains
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses with denylisted domains.

golden_categories.name: Blacklist with Regular Expressions
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses with domain blacklist using regular expressions.

golden_categories.name: Whitelisted Domains
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses with allowed domains.

golden_categories.name: Whitelist with Regular Expressions
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating email addresses with domain whitelist using regular expressions.

generated_categories.name: Basic Email Validation
generated_categories.description: Covers standard email validation with default options, ensuring compliance with common formats and rejection of invalid cases.

generated_categories.name: Display Name Handling
generated_categories.description: Tests email validation behavior when display names are allowed or required, ensuring proper parsing and rejection of invalid formats.

generated_categories.name: UTF-8 Local Part Handling
generated_categories.description: Validates behavior when UTF-8 characters are disallowed in the local part of the email address.

generated_categories.name: TLD Requirement Handling
generated_categories.description: Tests email validation when top-level domains (TLDs) are not required, allowing domains like 'localhost' or 'example'.

generated_categories.name: IP Address Domains
generated_categories.description: Covers validation when IP addresses are allowed in the domain part of the email.

generated_categories.name: Blacklisted Characters in Local Part
generated_categories.description: Tests validation when specific characters are blacklisted in the local part of the email address.

generated_categories.name: Blacklisted Domains
generated_categories.description: Validates behavior when specific domains are blacklisted, ensuring emails from these domains are rejected.

golden_to_generated.reason: Both categories focus on basic email validation, covering common formats and rejecting invalid cases.
No generated category specifically addresses domain-specific validation rules.
No generated category explicitly addresses underscores in the domain.
Both categories focus on disallowing UTF-8 characters in the local part of the email address.
GENC-2 covers display name handling broadly, but does not differentiate between optional and required display names.
GENC-2 includes tests for required display names but does not exclusively focus on this aspect.
Both categories validate email addresses with IPs allowed as domains.
Both categories focus on blacklisted characters in the local part of the email address.
No generated category explicitly addresses maximum length restrictions.
No generated category explicitly addresses ignoring maximum length restrictions.
GENC-7 covers blacklisted domains but does not differentiate between static and regex-based blacklists.
No generated category explicitly addresses regex-based domain blacklists.
No generated category explicitly addresses whitelisted domains.
No generated category explicitly addresses regex-based domain whitelists.
